proof_sketch: In the else branch (col>row), the original writes 0.0f to C[row*N+col]. The mutation writes 0.0f to C[row/N+col]. Since row is in [0, N-1] with N=4096, row/N = 0 for all valid rows. Thus the mutated index is simply col. The location C[col] corresponds to the element at row=0, col. For any (row, col) with col>row and row>0, the original writes to a distinct upper-triangular location, but the mutated writes to the first row (row=0). However, the upper-triangular elements (row>0, col>row) are never written by any thread in the mutated kernel, but they remain zero from the torch::zeros initialization. The first-row elements (row=0, col>0) are written by the else branch of the (0, col) thread, which also writes zero. All writes are zero; no output value changes. Hence the mutant is semantically equivalent.
